serviceDescription: AWS App Runner is a fully managed service that makes it easy for developers to quickly deploy containerized web applications and APIs, at scale and with no prior infrastructure experience required. Start with your source code or a container image. App Runner builds and deploys the web application automatically, load balances traffic with encryption, scales to meet your traffic needs, and makes it easy for your services to communicate with other AWS services and applications that run in a private Amazon VPC. With App Runner, rather than thinking about servers or scaling, you have more time to focus on your applications.

components.text: You pay for the vCPU and memory consumed by your active container instances as your application is processing requests. When your application is idle, you pay per GB of memory for provisioned container instances.

components.label: Container compute size
components.description: Choose the compute size for your container instance

components.label: Container memory size
components.description: Choose the memory size for your container instance

components.text: App Runner scales your active container instances up and down automatically to meet the processing demands of your application, and you pay only for the vCPU and memory used.

components.label: Concurrency
components.placeholder: Enter the number of requests per second
components.description: Enter the maximum number of simultaneous requests that can be processed by an active container instance.

components.label: Minimum provisioned container instances
components.description: Enter the minimum number of container instances you want to run for your application

components.label: Peak traffic hours
components.placeholder: Enter number of hours per day

components.label: Number of requests during peak traffic (requests/second)
components.placeholder: Enter number of requests received per second

components.label: Number of requests during off-peak traffic (requests/second)
components.placeholder: Enter number of requests received per second

operands.variableLabel: Number of requests during peak traffic (requests/second)
Number of requests during off-peak traffic (requests/second)

components.label: Auto deployment
components.description: Use automatic deployment for continuous integration and deployment (CICD). When App Runner dicovers a change to your source image or code, it automatically builds and deploys the new version to your App Runner service.

components.label: Build on App Runner
components.description: You will pay a build fee for the time it takes App Runner to build your application from your source code. You pay this build fee for the first deployment of your application and when there is a change made to the source code.

components.label: Code deployments per month
components.placeholder: Enter number of code deployments per month

components.label: Average build time (minutes)
components.placeholder: Enter average build time for your application

components.outputUnitLabel: select build on App Runner add on

operands.variableLabel: select build on App Runner add on

cards.title: Data Transfer
cards.helpText: You pay for all bandwidth into and out of AWS CodeArtifact, except for the following: 1. Data transferred in from the Internet. 2. Data transfer between CodeArtifact and another AWS Service in the same AWS Region

components.label: Data Transfer
components.title: Enter the data you expect to transfer out of $[Region]
